questions.question: Design a 3-to-8 line decoder using basic AND, OR, and NOT gates. Derive the truth table showing all 8 output combinations for inputs A, B, and C (where A is MSB). Write the Boolean expression for each output line Y0 through Y7.
keyPoints: Truth table must show all 8 input combinations (000 to 111) with active LOW or HIGH outputs
Derive minterms for each output: Y0 = A'·B'·C', Y1 = A'·B'·C, etc.
Use enable input concept (optional) for practical decoder implementation
Show that decoder generates all minterms from n input variables

questions.question: Implement a 4:1 multiplexer using only 2:1 multiplexers. Draw the connection diagram showing how the two select lines (S1, S0) are used, and verify the operation by writing the output expression Y = I0·S1'·S0' + I1·S1'·S0 + I2·S1·S0' + I3·S1·S0, where I0-I3 are data inputs.
keyPoints: Cascade two levels of 2:1 MUX - first level selects between input pairs, second level selects between results
Show proper connection of select lines: S0 to first level, S1 to second level
Derive complete Boolean expression showing data input routing based on select lines
Verify by constructing truth table with all 4 select combinations

questions.question: (a) Design a 4-to-2 priority encoder (with I3 having highest priority) with valid output indicator. Provide complete truth table showing all 16 input combinations, derive simplified Boolean expressions for outputs Y1, Y0 and valid signal V using Karnaugh maps. (b) Explain how two 4-to-2 priority encoders can be combined to form an 8-to-3 priority encoder with proper priority handling. Draw the block diagram and explain the role of the valid output in enabling the encoder at higher priority level.
keyPoints: Truth table must handle don't care conditions for lower priority inputs when higher priority is active
K-map simplification: Y1 = I3 + I2, Y0 = I3 + I1·I2', V = I0 + I1 + I2 + I3
For 8-to-3 encoder: use enable outputs and cascading - lower encoder handles I0-I3, upper handles I4-I7
Show how valid output of lower encoder enables upper encoder when all I0-I3 are inactive

questions.question: (a) Using a 3-to-8 decoder and external OR gates, design a circuit that implements the Boolean function F(A,B,C) = Σm(1,3,5,6) + d(2,7). Show the decoder outputs used and write the final expression. (b) Design a 16-to-1 multiplexer circuit to implement the function F(w,x,y,z) = Σm(0,2,5,7,8,10,13,15). Show how the variables are applied to select and data inputs, and identify any required literal generation for unused minterms.
keyPoints: Part (a): Connect decoder minterm outputs 1,3,5,6 to OR gate; handle don't cares by ORing minterms 2,7
Part (a): Final F = Y1 + Y3 + Y5 + Y6 (for active HIGH decoder outputs)
Part (b): Connect w,x,y,z as select lines (w=MSB, z=LSB), apply data inputs I0-I15 based on minterm values
Part (b): For unused minterms (1,3,4,6,9,11,12,14), connect to logic 0 or 1 based on don't care handling
